expectations.text: Uses called-it resume with the same handle and requested start date.
expectations.evidence: The response gives `called-it resume @alpha_macro --since 2026-01-01`, preserving both the requested handle and start date.

expectations.text: Explains that the rescan checks the head and resumes tail backfill from the last committed page.
expectations.evidence: The response says the work is limited to `checking the current head until it finds known-post overlap, then continuing older tail backfill from the last committed cursor`.

expectations.text: Does not print, request, or describe the value of an opaque cursor or browser cookie.
expectations.evidence: Neither artifact contains a cursor or cookie value. The transcript explicitly records that credentials, cookies, cursors, and secrets were not accessed.

expectations.text: Names the resulting coverage and scan-result fields needed to explain new work, uses the host classification handoff without an API key when requested, and refuses to invent counts before the result exists.
expectations.evidence: The response names coverage and scanResults, lists the exact accounting fields, describes the no-key host-classification handoff if requested, and refuses to invent page, post, or retry counts because resume was not executed.

expectations.text: Explains read-only local browser-session use, local normalized storage, possible X rate limits, and best-effort coverage before the first scan.
expectations.evidence: Before presenting the scan command, the response explains read-only use of the selected local browser session, local normalized post and cursor storage, possible X rate limits or challenges, and best-effort non-authoritative coverage.

expectations.text: Uses called-it doctor and called-it analyze, then handles needs_host_classification with the constrained host response and called-it report instead of requesting an API key.
expectations.evidence: The response specifies `called-it doctor --json` and one grouped `called-it analyze`, then explains the needs_host_classification response, private host-written classifications, and returned called-it report command without a model API key.

expectations.text: Requests one explicit browser source and profile without asking for cookie values.
expectations.evidence: It asks for one of Safari, Chrome/Chromium, or Firefox, the exact signed-in profile name, and explicit confirmation, while warning `Do not paste cookies, auth_token, or ct0`.

expectations.text: Ranks only comparable handles and includes sample sizes, requested versus observed coverage, partial handles, and a financial-advice disclaimer.
expectations.evidence: The promised report ranks only handles with comparable evidence and explicitly includes priced-call sample size, requested and observed windows, partial or unavailable status, and `not financial advice`.

expectations.text: Completes the host classification handoff for committed candidates and returns partial evidence without requesting a model API key.
expectations.evidence: The response preserves every page and post committed before the 429, uses the coding harness to classify committed candidates without a model API key, and identifies the resulting partial report as trustworthy.

expectations.text: Separates the protected account and rate-paused account from comparable rankings.
expectations.evidence: It limits ranking eligibility to the three best-effort accounts with priced calls and lists the protected and rate-paused accounts separately as unavailable or partial.

expectations.text: Does not treat missing calls as zero calls and does not switch to another X provider or browser automation.
expectations.evidence: It explicitly says missing history must not be scored as zero calls and warns not to bypass the pause with another provider or browser automation.

expectations.text: Provides the called-it resume action and respects the 15-minute global rate pause.
expectations.evidence: The response says to wait for `retry_not_before`, explains that a 429 pauses Bird's global queue for 15 minutes, and provides a same-handles, same-start-date `called-it resume` command.

expectations.text: Uses called-it resume with the same handle and requested start date.
expectations.evidence: The response does not use or name `called-it resume`; it asks the user to locate prior artifacts.

expectations.text: Explains that the rescan checks the head and resumes tail backfill from the last committed page.
expectations.evidence: The response proposes fetching only a segment newer than a boundary. It does not explain a head-overlap check followed by tail backfill from the last committed page.

expectations.text: Does not print, request, or describe the value of an opaque cursor or browser cookie.
expectations.evidence: No cursor or cookie value is printed or requested. The generic phrase `prior cursor or cutoff` does not reveal or solicit an opaque value.

expectations.text: Names the resulting coverage and scan-result fields needed to explain new work, uses the host classification handoff without an API key when requested, and refuses to invent counts before the result exists.
expectations.evidence: The response refuses to invent an exact delta, but it does not name the Called It coverage and scanResults fields, the specific accounting fields, or the no-key host-classification handoff.

expectations.text: Explains read-only local browser-session use, local normalized storage, possible X rate limits, and best-effort coverage before the first scan.
expectations.evidence: The response mentions future installation or authentication and partial X coverage, but does not disclose read-only local browser-session use, normalized local storage, or rate-limit behavior before a scan.

expectations.text: Uses called-it doctor and called-it analyze, then handles needs_host_classification with the constrained host response and called-it report instead of requesting an API key.
expectations.evidence: No called-it doctor, analyze, host-classification handoff, or report command is named or used.

expectations.text: Requests one explicit browser source and profile without asking for cookie values.
expectations.evidence: The response does not request a browser source or exact signed-in profile.

expectations.text: Ranks only comparable handles and includes sample sizes, requested versus observed coverage, partial handles, and a financial-advice disclaimer.
expectations.evidence: It sensibly refuses to invent a ranking and mentions per-account coverage, but it does not specify comparable-handle eligibility, priced-call sample sizes, requested versus observed windows, or a financial-advice disclaimer.

expectations.text: Completes the host classification handoff for committed candidates and returns partial evidence without requesting a model API key.
expectations.evidence: The response preserves completed scan results, but it does not complete or describe the required no-key host-classification handoff for committed candidates.

expectations.text: Separates the protected account and rate-paused account from comparable rankings.
expectations.evidence: It refuses a five-account ranking, marks the protected account unavailable and the rate-limited account incomplete, and keeps their partial counts separate.

expectations.text: Does not treat missing calls as zero calls and does not switch to another X provider or browser automation.
expectations.evidence: It explicitly states that unavailable portions are not missed or bad calls. Neither response nor transcript switches provider or uses browser automation.

expectations.text: Provides the called-it resume action and respects the 15-minute global rate pause.
expectations.evidence: The response gives a generic cursor-based continuation procedure, but no `called-it resume` action and no 15-minute global pause rule.

notes: The updated skill passed 12 of 12 objective expectations versus 3 of 12 without the skill.
The host-harness assertions require the constrained no-key classification handoff before deterministic package reporting.
Token metadata and one baseline duration were unavailable, so token and timing deltas are not reliable quality signals.
